nodes.title: ETA (Method Sigma High Noise)
widgets_values:
# 🎚️ Adjusting ETA

**HIGHER ETA**  
→ smoother noise  
→ less detail

**LOWER ETA**  
→ more detail  
→ more visible noise

nodes.title: Denoise (Method Sigma High Noise)
widgets_values:
# 🎚️ Adjusting Noise Reduction

**Noise Reduction** controls how realistic the result can become:

- The **higher** the value, the **more realistic** the output will be (if the prompt supports it).
- **radau_ia_2s** generally provides **greater realism** than the initial **Euler** sampler.

nodes.title: First Sampling Step (Method Sigma High Noise)
widgets_values:
# 🔢 First Sampling Step

Set this value to **around 4**.  
You may increase it slightly if you are using a **high number of steps**.

Adjust this parameter just enough to obtain a **good initial image composition** in the first sampling preview.

nodes.title: Steps (Method Sigma High Noise)
widgets_values:
# 🔢 Step Behavior Explanation

Minimum **8 steps** are required.  
Changing the step count here will create **false emulated steps**, because the **FlowMatch Euler Discrete Scheduler** on the left determines the actual number of effective steps.

However, increasing the step value still influences the behavior:

• Setting **30 steps** in this node  
→ produces a result similar to about **20 actual steps**.

In short:  
The step slider here *does not define real step count*, but it *affects how many effective steps the scheduler will simulate*, creating a proportional but reduced number of real steps.

nodes.title: ⚙️ Method: Sigma Low Noise
widgets_values:
# ⚙️ Method: Sigma Low Noise

This method uses a custom sigma curve to achieve more controlled denoising, producing a **softer** result while still maintaining good detail.

## 🔁 Scheduler & Sampler
• The **scheduler is user-selectable**.  
• The **final sampler is also user-selectable**, but not every sampler–scheduler pairing will behave well.  
  It is normal if certain combinations fail or produce weak results.

## 🔧 Sigma Modifiers
• **Multiply Sigmas (stateless)** modifies part of the sigma curve by multiplying it with a factor, affecting how noise evolves across specific step ranges.  
• **Detail Daemon Sampler** enhances detail in selected regions of the sigma curve by adjusting the sampling behavior locally.

## 🧩 User Options
The user can:  
• choose the seed  
• choose the number of steps  
• enable or disable Multiply Sigmas (stateless)  
• enable or disable Detail Daemon Sampler  

Together, these controls allow precise tuning of softness, detail and overall character.

## 🎯 Purpose
This method is designed for workflows where **sigma control** is essential, giving fine control over the noise structure while keeping a **clean, low-noise appearance**.

```
# 📂 ComfyUI Workflow  
├── Scheduler (user-selectable)  
│     └── generates the sigma curve  
├── Multiply Sigmas (stateless) (optional)  
├── Detail Daemon Sampler (optional)  
├── Sampler (user-selectable)  
│     └── Seed + Steps  
└── Output → low-noise, controlled denoising result
```

nodes.title: 🔀 SeedVarianceEnhancer
widgets_values:
# 🔀 SeedVarianceEnhancer

https://github.com/ChangeTheConstants/SeedVarianceEnhancer

**SeedVarianceEnhancer** is a ComfyUI custom node designed to increase output diversity for **Z-Image Turbo**.  
It compensates for **low seed variance**, a situation where images look too similar even when using different seeds.  
It works by injecting random noise into the **embedding** during the early steps of generation.

# 🔧 Parameter Explanation

## 🎲 randomize_percent
Determines what percentage of embedding values are randomly selected to be modified with noise.

## 💪 Strength
Controls the intensity of the injected noise.  
Higher strength → stronger variation.

## 🎯 Seed
Controls which embedding values are modified and how the noise behaves.

## 🌫️ Noise_insert
Default: **"noise on beginning steps"**  
This adds noise only early in the process, then removes it, allowing the model to return toward prompt fidelity at the end.  
Setting it to **"noise on all steps"** will significantly reduce prompt adherence and worsen text rendering.

## 🔄 steps_switchover_percent
Determines the percentage of total steps processed before switching between noisy and original embeddings.

Formula to compute an appropriate value:  
**(100 / TOTAL_STEPS) * STEPS – 1**

---

# 🎚️ Recommended Settings for Z-Image Turbo

• **Strength:** 15 → 30  
• **randomize_percent:** 50  
• **steps_switchover_percent:** default is 20%

These values are good starting points. Adjust depending on your prompt.

---

# 📝 Notes on Prompt Behavior

• Some prompts benefit from higher or lower step switchover settings.  
• Longer, more detailed prompts give SeedVarianceEnhancer **more embedding values to modify**.  
• Counterintuitively, **more detailed prompts can produce more diverse outputs**, because there is more structure to manipulate.

nodes.title: ⚙️ Method: Sigma High Noise
widgets_values:
# ⚙️ Method: Sigma High Noise

This method uses a two-pass sampling strategy combining KSampler (Advanced) for the first pass and ClownsharKSampler for the second. It uses the sigma curve from FlowMatch Euler Discrete Scheduler (Custom). The result tends to look more intense, sharper and more detailed, hence the name High Noise.

## 🔁 Sigma Generation
Sigmas are generated by the FlowMatch Euler Discrete Scheduler (Custom), recommended for Z-Image. They can optionally be modified by Multiply Sigmas (stateless), which the user can enable or disable.

# 1️⃣ First Pass — KSampler (Advanced)
• Uses KSampler (Advanced).  
• Recommended: Sampler = Euler, Scheduler = FlowMatch Euler Discrete Scheduler.  
• The user can choose the seed.  
• The first end step defines where this pass stops. Recommended value: around 4, slightly higher for very high step counts.

# 2️⃣ Second Pass — ClownsharKSampler
• User defines the number of steps. Minimum recommended: 8.  
• These steps behave as “fake emulated steps”: the real count comes from the FlowMatch Euler Discrete Scheduler.  
  Example: setting 30 steps behaves like ~20 real steps.

## 🎚️ ETA Control
• Higher ETA → smoother noise, less detail.  
• Lower ETA → more detail, but more visible noise.

## 🎚️ Denoise Control
Higher denoise values can increase realism, especially with samplers like radau_ia_2s, which may look more realistic than the initial Euler pass.

## 🔥 Purpose
This method targets users who want more sharpness, clarity and punch, accepting a more aggressive high-noise behavior.

# 📂 Example Node Structure

```
📂 ComfyUI Workflow  
├── FlowMatch Euler Discrete Scheduler (Custom)  
│     └── generates sigma curve  
├── Multiply Sigmas (stateless) (optional)  
├── KSampler (Advanced)  
│     ├── Sampler: Euler  
│     ├── Scheduler: FlowMatch Euler Discrete Scheduler  
│     ├── Seed: user-defined  
│     └── First End Step: ~4  
└── ClownsharKSampler  
      ├── Steps: min 8  
      ├── ETA: user-defined  
      └── Denoise: user-defined
```

nodes.title: Model links
widgets_values:
## Model links

**text_encoders**

- [qwen_3_4b.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_3_4b.safetensors)

**diffusion_models**

- [z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/diffusion_models/z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors)

**vae**

- [ae.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/vae/ae.safetensors) alternative is: flux dev vae

**ControlNet**

- [Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/alibaba-pai/Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union/resolve/main/Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union.safetensors)


Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │      └── qwen_3_4b.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │      └── z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │      └── ae.safetensors
│   └── 📂 model_patches/
│          └── Z-Image-Turbo-Fun Controlnet-Union.safetensors
```

nodes.title: 📝 Source Prompt
widgets_values:
# 📝 Source Prompt

**Source Prompt** lets you choose where the prompt for your image generation comes from.

**Positive**  
The manual method — you are fully in control and write your own prompt.

**Local Media Manager**  
Allows you to use your own images to import their prompts, as long as the images contain the required metadata.

**Civitai Discovery Hub**  
Lets you pick an image from Civitai and extract its positive prompt, negative prompt, metadata and the image itself.  

**QwenVL**  
Uses an LLM to analyze a loaded image and automatically generate a descriptive prompt.  
Choose the appropriate model depending on your computer’s capabilities.

nodes.title: Image Megapixels (SeedVR2)
widgets_values:
# 🖼️ Source Image Megapixels

**Default value:** 0.27 MP

# 🖼️ Target Image Megapixels

**Range:** 1.7 → 8.85  
**Recommended:** 6.0 MP

nodes.title: 🔍 SeedVR2 Overview
widgets_values:
# 🔍 SeedVR2 Overview

SeedVR2 is a powerful upscaling tool designed for situations where the **source images are very small** and the **target resolutions are extremely high**.  
It excels at enriching and refining low-resolution visuals.

# 🖼️ Source Image Size and Quality

• Ideal source size: **0.1 to 1 megapixel**  
• A highly effective common value: **0.27 MP (512 × 512)**  
  → good balance between added detail and preserved texture  
• Larger source images keep more of their original texture, but SeedVR2 will introduce slightly fewer new details.

# 📈 Output Image Resolution & VRAM

• Increasing the **target resolution** usually improves the final result.  
• Around **6 megapixels** often produces especially strong outcomes.  
• The **console log** provides detailed VRAM usage information, helping you adjust settings when necessary.

nodes.title: 📝 Note About ControlNet
widgets_values:
# 📝 Note About ControlNet

When you are **not** using ControlNet, make sure to **disable the preprocessor** in the ControlNet settings.  
Otherwise, it will run unnecessarily and consume processing time for no benefit.

nodes.title: 🔧 Image Apply LUT
widgets_values:
# 🔧 Using LUTs with the “Image Apply LUT” Node

You can download LUTs from the internet to use with the Image Apply LUT node.

I personally recommend **NaturalBoost.cube** and **Presetpro – Agfa Vista.cube**, although I haven’t tested them all yet. If you find good LUTs, feel free to share them in the comments.

## 📎 Useful Links
• [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Kf9caLESnaUIei8-_dgg7OlKUdsvIFEE/view](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Kf9caLESnaUIei8-_dgg7OlKUdsvIFEE/view)  
• [https://www.freepresets.com/download/free-lut-agfa-vista-pro-24/](https://www.freepresets.com/download/free-lut-agfa-vista-pro-24/)


## 📁 Installation Path
Place your `.cube` LUT files in:
\custom_nodes\comfyui_essentials\luts

## 📝 What Are LUTs?
LUTs (Lookup Tables) apply a predefined color style to your image. They adjust:
• color tones  
• contrast  
• overall mood  
They work like filters but with higher precision and a more professional result.

```

# LUT Storage Location

📂 ComfyUI/  
└── 📂 custom_nodes/  
  └── 📂 comfyui_essentials/  
    └── 📂 luts/  
      ├── NaturalBoost.cube  
      ├── Agfa_Vista.cube  
      └── your_other_LUTs_here.cube

```

nodes.title: 🎨 Generation Inpaint (SAM2 Segmentation)
widgets_values:
# 🎨 Generation Inpaint (SAM2 Segmentation)

This method allows you to automatically define which part of the image should be selected for inpainting by filling in the **“SAM2 Segmentation (RMBG)”** field.  
For example, entering **“face”** will select only the face.

You can use keywords such as:  
**face**, **person**, **clothing**, **background**, etc.

Once the generation is finished, the selected region—whether chosen manually through inpaint or automatically via SAM2 Segmentation—will appear in the **“Preview SAM2 Segmentation / Inpaint”** node.

nodes.title: 👁️ Workflow Display Recommendation
widgets_values:
# 👁️ Workflow Display Recommendation

This workflow was designed to be used with **node link visualization disabled**.  
Disabling link display provides:

• a **cleaner and clearer visual layout**  
• **better performance**, especially in large workflows  

Link visualization is mainly useful when you need to **modify or extend** the workflow structure.

nodes.title: Image preparation
widgets_values:
## Why pre-process an image before upscaling?

Image upscalers (like SeedVR2) don’t “create” detail out of thin air, they **reconstruct** missing information by reading cues from the input: edges, local contrast, tiny texture variations, and overall structure.  
If the source image is a bit soft, overly smooth, or lacks micro-contrast, the upscaler has fewer clear signals to follow, which can lead to a flatter, less defined result.

This preparation stage is meant to **optimize the input signal** before upscaling:
- **Sharpening (adaptive deblur)** increases edge clarity and micro-contrast, helping the model reconstruct shapes more cleanly.
- **Film grain (micro-texture)** adds subtle variation that can prevent overly uniform surfaces and encourage richer reconstruction.
- **Light JPEG simulation** can introduce a very subtle micro-structure that some models may interpret as texture information.

### Excluding specific areas (e.g. hair) with SAM2
This image preparation can sometimes make **hair look too smooth** and less natural.  
To avoid that, you can exclude a specific region using segmentation:

- Enable **`No noise to select mask`** and choose **`hair`**
- Feed that selection into **`No noise to zone (SAM2 Segmentation)`**

This way, the preparation will **not be applied** to the selected area (hair), while remaining active on the rest of the image.

### Disable it when it’s not needed
- Disable **`No noise to select mask`** and **`Image preparation`** when you are not using **SeedVR2** upscaling, so they don’t run for nothing during normal generations.
- You can also disable them anytime, including for upscaling, if you simply don’t want to use this preparation stage.

nodes.title: FilmGrain
widgets_values:
## FilmGrain

**Purpose**  
Adds a light micro-texture before upscaling.  
Here, the goal is not to keep a visible grain after the upscale, but to provide a slightly richer input signal, which can help the upscaler reconstruct fine detail and avoid an overly uniform look.

**Effect on upscaling**  
- Low intensity can improve the perceived texture after upscaling.
- Too much grain can strongly influence reconstruction and change the overall look.

**Parameters**  
- `intensity`: amount of micro-texture.  
  Higher = stronger effect.
- `scale`: size of the micro-texture.  
  Lower = finer, higher = larger.
- `temperature`, `vignette`: aesthetic controls; for technical pre-processing they are often kept at `0`.

**Presets**  
- **Clean / Universal**: `intensity = 0.02`, `scale = 25`, `temperature = 0`, `vignette = 0`
- **Very light (subtle prep)**: `intensity = 0.01`, `scale = 25`
- **Texture+ (more noticeable)**: `intensity = 0.03–0.05`, `scale = 25–35`
- **Portrait (softer)**: `intensity = 0.01–0.02`, `scale = 25–35`

nodes.title: 👑 FRED Image Sharpening Blur Level
widgets_values:
## 👑 FRED Image Sharpening Blur Level

**Purpose**  
Prepares the image for upscaling by automatically increasing sharpness when the input is considered too “soft”.  
This gives the upscaler cleaner edges and stronger micro-contrast, which often helps it reconstruct details.

**How it works**  
- The node measures a sharpness score (edge-based).
- If the score is already high enough, it outputs the image unchanged.
- Otherwise, it applies progressive sharpening until the target threshold is reached.

**Effect on upscaling**  
- Makes edges and micro-contrast clearer before the upscale, which often leads to a sharper result.
- Pushing it too high can make the image look harder and over-emphasize some textures.

**Parameter**  
- `minimum_deblur_output` (0–100): target sharpness threshold.  
  Higher value = stronger intervention.

**Presets**  
- **Clean / Universal**: `minimum_deblur_output = 50`
- **Soft input (more bite)**: `minimum_deblur_output = 60`
- **Max detail (aggressive, use rarely)**: `minimum_deblur_output = 70`
- **Portrait (softer)**: `minimum_deblur_output = 45–55`

nodes.title: Presets complets
widgets_values:
## Full presets (all 3 nodes together)

### Clean preset (recommended)
- Deblur: `minimum_deblur_output = 50`
- FilmGrain: `intensity = 0.02`, `scale = 25`, `temperature = 0`, `vignette = 0`
- JPEG: `quality = 95`

### Detail+ preset (if the input lacks bite)
- Deblur: `minimum_deblur_output = 60`
- FilmGrain: `intensity = 0.02`, `scale = 25`, `temperature = 0`, `vignette = 0`
- JPEG: `quality = 92`

### Portrait preset (softer)
- Deblur: `minimum_deblur_output = 45–55`
- FilmGrain: `intensity = 0.01–0.02`, `scale = 25–35`, `temperature = 0`, `vignette = 0`
- JPEG: `quality = 95–98` (or disabled if you want ultra-clean output)

nodes.title: 👑 FRED Jpeg Artifact Simulator
widgets_values:
## 👑 FRED Jpeg Artifact Simulator

**Purpose**  
Simulates JPEG compression in memory (compress + reload).  
At high quality, the effect is subtle and can add a small amount of micro-structure that the upscaler may interpret as detail. At lower quality, it becomes a clearly stylized “compressed” look.

**Effect on upscaling**  
- Higher `quality`: very light “patina”, may slightly stimulate some textures.
- Lower `quality`: more visible compression look, stronger impact on the final result.

**Parameter**  
- `quality` (1–100): JPEG quality.  
  Higher = fewer artifacts.

**Presets**  
- **Clean / Universal**: `quality = 95`
- **Subtle but a bit stronger**: `quality = 92`
- **Texture / light stylization**: `quality = 85–90`
- **Compressed look (lo-fi)**: `quality = 70–85`

nodes.title: FaceDetailer – Targeted Detail Enhancement
widgets_values:
## FaceDetailer – Targeted Detail Enhancement

### Description

FaceDetailer nodes are used to enhance specific areas of an image by applying a localized refinement pass instead of reprocessing the entire image.

In this workflow, detection is handled through **Ultralytics (YOLO)** models using the **UltralyticsDetectorProvider** node. These models automatically detect predefined regions, allowing FaceDetailer to apply additional detail only where needed.

This approach helps improve fine details on sensitive or complex areas while preserving the overall consistency of the image.

---

### Detection Models

Ultralytics detection models are fully **user-selectable**. You are free to use any compatible models from Civitai, HuggingFace, or other sources.

In this workflow, the following model is used:

- **Eyeful_v2-Paired**  
  Recommended for eye detection, as it provides robust and reliable eye detection and works very well with FaceDetailer in ComfyUI.  
  [Eyeful_v2-Paired (Civitai)](https://civitai.com/models/178518/eyeful-or-robust-eye-detection-for-adetailer-comfyui)

- **face_yolov8m.pt**  
  Recommended for face detection when you want higher detection reliability (more accurate, but heavier than smaller YOLO variants).  
  [Download (HuggingFace)](https://huggingface.co/xingren23/comfyflow-models/tree/main/ultralytics/bbox)


---

### Model Installation Path

All Ultralytics models must be placed in the following directory:



```
📁 ComfyUI
└── 📁 models
    └── 📁 ultralytics
        └── 📁 bbox
            ├── 📄 face_yolov8m.pt
            └── 📄 Eyeful_v2-Paired.pt
```


After copying the models into this folder, restart ComfyUI so they can be detected correctly.

---

### Workflow Behavior

1. **UltralyticsDetectorProvider** detects the target regions based on the selected model.
2. **FaceDetailer** applies a refinement pass only on the detected areas.
3. The rest of the image remains unchanged, preserving global coherence.
4. Each FaceDetailer node can be enabled, disabled, or adjusted independently.

nodes.title: 💾 Save Settings
widgets_values:
# 💾 Save Settings

Make sure you are using **the forked version** of **ComfyUI-Image-Saver** available here:  
👉 [https://github.com/alexopus/ComfyUI-Image-Saver](https://github.com/alexopus/ComfyUI-Image-Saver)

**Save settings** allow you to precisely define how your images are saved.

It is strongly recommended to use **Save Image Final**, which saves the image **at the very end of the workflow**, once **all filters have been applied**.

**Note:**  
The **1st pass** and **upscale** options **do not include** the applied filters.

---

## 🧩 Metadata Saving

Nodes configured this way automatically save:
- the **model used**,
- **LoRA** along with their **weights/strengths**,
- the **prompt**,
- the **generation parameters**.

The generated **metadata is directly compatible with Civitai**, allowing immediate import, sharing, and reading of the information without any additional handling.

This ensures a complete, clear, and easily reusable history for every generated image.

nodes.title: 🧩 Workflow Modularity
widgets_values:
# 🧩 Workflow Modularity

This workflow was designed to be **fully modular**, allowing you to easily add your own generation modes, generation methods, upscale methods, and prompt sources.

---

# 🔧 Adding a **Generation Mode**

To add a new generation mode:

1. Create a **group** inside the **“Generation Preview”** subgraph.  
2. The group name must contain the word **“generation”**.  
3. The group must be **cyan-colored** so it can be detected by the **“Generation Mode”** node, which enables or disables unused modes automatically.  
4. Connect the **outputs** of your mode to the appropriate **switch nodes**.

---

# ⚙️ Adding a **Generation Method**

1. Create a **group** inside the **“Generation Preview”** subgraph.  
2. The group name must contain the word **“method”**.  
3. Properly connect the **inputs** to the switches and pipes.  
4. Connect the **latent output** to the **“Latent Switch 2”** node.

---

# 🔍 Adding an **Upscale Method**

1. Create a **group** inside the **“Upscale”** subgraph.  
2. The group name must contain the word **“Upscale”**.  
3. The group must be **green-colored** to be detected.  
4. Connect the **inputs from the pipe** and the **image output** to the **“Image Switch Upscale”** node.

---

# 📝 Adding a **Prompt Source**

To add a new prompt source:

1. Connect the **text output** of your source to the **“Prompt P Switch”** node, located on the right side of the workflow inside the **“Backend”** group.  
2. Link your node to **“Source Prompt”**, allowing the system to **disable your source automatically** when another prompt source is selected.

---

This system ensures easy, clean, and fully controlled extensibility for your workflow.

nodes.title: 📊 Recommended Samplers & Schedulers
widgets_values:
# 📊 Recommended Samplers & Schedulers for Z-Image

| **Sampler**                       | **Recommended Scheduler(s)**             |
|-----------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| **seeds_2**, **seeds_3**          | **bong_tangent**, **Beta**                |
| **euler_ancestral**               | **Beta**, **ddim_uniform**, **FlowMatchEulerDiscreteScheduler**                |
| **dpmpp_sde**                     | **Beta**, **ddim_uniform**                |
| **dpmpp_2s_ancestral**            | **FlowMatchEulerDiscreteScheduler**       |
| **sa_solver**                     | **simple**                                |
| **deis**                          | **Beta**                                  |
| **res_2s**                        | **Beta**                                  |

---

# 📝 Notes & Required Extensions

• To use **FlowMatchEulerDiscreteScheduler**, install:  
  [https://github.com/erosDiffusion/ComfyUI-EulerDiscreteScheduler](https://github.com/erosDiffusion/ComfyUI-EulerDiscreteScheduler)

• To access the **res** sampler and the **bong_tangent** scheduler, install:  
  [https://github.com/ClownsharkBatwing/RES4LYF](https://github.com/ClownsharkBatwing/RES4LYF)
  

• **sa_solver** requires **more steps** than usual for **optimal results.**

• Each sampler–scheduler combination can require **more or fewer steps** depending on the result you want.  
  For example, **euler_ancestral + Beta** can perform very well with **20 steps** in some cases.

nodes.title: ⚠️ Note: Inpaint & Img2Img Compatibility
widgets_values:
# ⚠️ Note: Inpaint & Img2Img Compatibility

Img2Img and Inpaint are **NOT compatible with Method: Sigma High Noise**.  
They work normally with all other methods.

nodes.title: 👤 Face Detailer - Face
widgets_values:
# 👤 Face Detailer - Face

The **Face Detailer** works similarly to a refiner but applies **only to the face**.  
It is especially useful when the face is **far from the camera**, helping recover details that are usually lost at a distance.

# 🔧 Recommended Settings

## 🔢 Steps
**6 to 12**  
Choose more steps for higher precision.

## 🎚️ CFG
Keep at **1.0**.

## 🧪 Sampler
**LCM**

## 🗓️ Scheduler
**Karras**, **Exponential**, or **Beta**

## 🎨 Denoise
The ideal denoise value depends on how close the face is to the camera:

• **Close face:** keep it **low** → **0.07 to 0.15**  
• **Distant face:** use a **higher** value → **0.20 to 0.35**  

You can experiment to find the best fit for your workflow.  
If you prefer a simple and stable option, setting it to **0.15** works well in most cases.

nodes.title: ⚠️ Changelog 1.20
widgets_values:
## Changelog 1.20

### ⚠️ Important Notice
This workflow **is not designed to be used with _Nodes 2.0_ mode**.

To use the **Z-Image** model, you must:
- select the **CLIP type**: `Lumina2`
- use **only one CLIP**, for example:  
  `qwen_3_4b.safetensors`

---

### 🔧 Changes and Improvements

- **Complete overhaul of image saving**, still **fully compatible with Civitai**.  
  Uses **ComfyUI-Image-Saver**.  
  Make sure you are using **this forked version**:  
  👉 [https://github.com/alexopus/ComfyUI-Image-Saver](https://github.com/alexopus/ComfyUI-Image-Saver)

- **Added Eye Detailer**.  
  Added a note *“FaceDetailer – Targeted Detail Enhancement”* indicating:
  - the **required models**,
  - their **installation locations**.

- **Improved SeedVR2 upscaling**, with image preparation before upscaling.  
  Check the model used in **“SeedVR2 (Down)Load DiT Model”** and select the one compatible with your PC configuration.  
  If your system allows it, you can keep the currently used model.  
  👉 **New notes are now available** to help guide the configuration.

- **Added 🧱 Universal Latent Settings**.  
  This node integrates **all officially supported resolutions for the Z-Image model**, ensuring correct latent sizes and improved compatibility.

- **Added prompt preview support** for:
  - **Local Media Manager**
  - **🖼️ Civitai Discovery Hub**

- **Updated the 🖼️ Civitai Discovery Hub node** (please make sure to update it).  
  This version fixes:
  - automatic scrolling bugs,
  - additional image loading when the user left the node view and then returned.

  The code has been **completely rewritten** to comply with **ComfyUI V3**.

nodes.title: Credits
widgets_values:
# 📘 Credits

“**Method Sigma High Noise**” come from **Luneva’s workflow** on Civitai, which I slightly adapted with a few modifications.

Special thanks to him for his excellent work and for his **Midjourney Luneva Cinematic LoRA**, which I truly appreciate.

Link to the original workflow:  
https://civitai.com/models/2185167?modelVersionId=2465011

nodes.title: 📘 Credits
widgets_values:

https://civitai.com/models/1376005/photoflow-z-image-turbo-qwen-chroma-wan-2221-sdxl-t2i-text-to-image-txt2img-workflow?modelVersionId=2478301
